item.title: Re: SCP-4441
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback and your take on the article! That repeated "range" was a typo, I fixed it. I'm European so I was blissfully unaware that A4 isn't the standard paper format in USA. I think I'll leave that as it is.

Regarding your questions, here's what was going on with SCP-6762 in this story-within-a-story, in my opinion:

+author interpretation
– hide block

The unindentified glyph covering the face of SCP-6762 is a powerful cognitohazard that all information regarding it will be percieved differently by everyone who sees it. The effect of this glyph extends to SCP-6762 itself. All researchers assigned to SCP-6762 therefore had a different perception of the entity, which caused conflicting Special Containment Procedures to be written for it.

One of these conflicting views caused the research staff to believe that SCP-6762 must be routinely "appeased" by feeding it dead D-class personnel. This was false - SCP-6762 would technically constitute as a Safe Object. Not only that, but 6762's antimemetic property is strengthened by consuming brain tissue. Therefore, the Containment Procedures only served to exacerbate the situation.

The Foundation kept feeding SCP-6762 bodies until its antimemetic range reached zero. If starved for brain matter, SCP-6762's antimemetic aura would shrink and eventually dissipate.

SCP-6762 being 2.5 meters tall and vocalising on a near-inaubly low frequency was a jab towards cringy anomalous humanoids that are super tall and mysterious and have a deep voice.

Meanwhile, SCP-4441-1 being small, asocial and artistically productive is a reference towards myself, and new SCP writers in general. And whadya know: SCP-4441-1 also writes skips.

item.title: Re: SCP-4441
item.content:encoded:
I really like this :) previous crit deleted as I was being a derp (the MTF steamroller happens during intial containment and the termination attempt is presumably the option of nuking the site)

It seems like the "foundation is made to look foolish" option may also apply, given that they ended up being spooked into launching a wide scale recapture attempt… thanks to nothing more than
+ show block
– hide block

the fake documents pooped out by a monkey with a printer in place of its guts XD (which might furthermore count as "thing that's too good at what it does", because if its printouts were less convincing or less hardwearing it might never have been captured…)

Also, was "vocalizations on the range of 8 to 30Hz range" deliberate, to suggest whoever/whatever was making the documents was fallible, or just a typo? There's the obvious repeated word, but "on" should probably be "in" too. Similarly, are the A4 printouts for weirdness' sake, given that a north American organisation (and anyone local faking the documents on purpose) would more likely use the slightly different 8.5 x 11 inch "Letter" size paper?

NB I sort of hope the actual spooky scary murder monster finds its way into the mainlist under another number at some point, because that's actually a pretty neat idea for it. Is the implication also that its area of visibility tightens up the more you get used to seeing it, or the more stressed/hungry it gets? That could make it quite difficult to keep observation of in containment, particularly if the radius ends up tightening up so much that you'd have to hug it to perceive it (or moreso if the radius ends up inside its own body), making it functionally invisible/imperceptible… Though maybe automated systems would be able to detect it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could have been Memetic Resistance Index. Though maybe MRQ (Memetic Resistance Quotient) might be more sciencey? :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed it from MRI to MRV. I don't think it's used on any other article, but at least it's nternally consistent now. Thanks for letting me know!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Indeed, I was expecting something different from an antimemetic slaughtermonster.

+1 for clever execution

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really did not see that coming! Nice article that was very unique.

item.title: SCP-4441
item.content:encoded:
My first SCP, and also an entry for the Cliché Contest 2019 under the categories:
Cliché anomaly:

  • Spooky scary murder monster

Cliché story elements:

  • A highly skilled MTF gets steamrolled
  • Termination attempts

Image source is a public domain image from GoodFreePhotos.com

Thanks for RockTeethMothEyes for reviewing it and offering constructive criticism.
